pss.name: Promoting Skills for Success
pss.description: Empowering Youth through Digital Skills and Education

footer.copy: © 2026 Promoting Skills for Success Cambodia. All rights reserved.

hero.title: Empowering Cambodian Youth through Skills and Education
hero.subtitle: Promoting Skills for Success (PSS) Cambodia is a local non-governmental organization registered with the Ministry of Interior of Cambodia. We are committed to fighting poverty by unlocking the potential of vulnerable youth through providing access to digital education.

about.title: Fighting Poverty through Digital Education
about.subtitle: PSS is a local implementing partner of Passerelles Numériques Cambodia (PNC) which started in 2005 in Phnom Penh and has provided a two-year ICT training program for underprivileged youth. The program covers software development, soft skills, English language, health activities, and job search skills support. PSS also provides technical support for NomadLab, a free tool for youth club members, students, teachers, and alumni to expand digital access and build digital skills.

impact.description: At PSS, in partnership with other organizations, we believe that real impact is measured by real outcomes: the skills acquired, the transformed futures, and the strengthened communities. Our programs provide opportunities for youth, especially those from rural and underprivileged backgrounds, with technical skills and career support to thrive in the digital economy.
impact.quote: "With full support for housing, food, materials, and sufficient health costs, students can focus on their studies and build a sustainable future."
impact.stat1_sub: Since 2005 following PNC's 20 years of experience
impact.stat4_sub: Closing the gender gap in the tech sector
